Customer.title: Customer
Customer.description: Represents a customer of the internet service, storing their personal details, contact information, and assigned service package.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Customer entity.

name.description: The full name of the customer.

address.description: The physical address of the customer.

phoneNumber.description: The contact phone number of the customer.

email.description: The email address of the customer.

servicePackageId.description: Reference to the ServicePackage entity. (Relationship: ServicePackage 1:N Customer)

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the customer record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the customer record was last updated.

ServicePackage.title: ServicePackage
ServicePackage.description: Defines the different internet service packages offered, including their names, prices, and features.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ServicePackage entity.

name.description: The name of the service package (e.g., 'Basic Fiber 20Mbps').

price.description: The monthly price of the service package.

description.description: A detailed description of the features included in the service package.

isActive.description: A flag indicating whether the service package is currently active and available for new customers.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the service package record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the service package record was last updated.

Invoice.title: Invoice
Invoice.description: Records monthly invoices generated for customers and tracks their payment status, amount due, and payment dates.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Invoice entity.

customerId.description: Reference to the Customer entity for whom this invoice was generated. (Relationship: Customer 1:N Invoice)

invoiceDate.description: The date when the invoice was generated.

dueDate.description: The date by which the invoice payment is due.

amountDue.description: The total amount of money owed for this invoice.

paymentDate.description: The date when the payment for this invoice was received (if paid).

paymentStatus.description: The current status of the invoice payment (e.g., 'pending', 'paid', 'overdue').

notes.description: Any additional notes or remarks about the invoice or payment.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the invoice record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the invoice record was last updated.

definition.description: Stores definitions of internet service packages, including names, prices, and features. Accessed by authenticated users (read) and administrators (read/write).

params.name: servicePackageId
params.description: Unique identifier for a specific service package.

definition.description: Contains detailed information about each customer. Includes denormalized 'linkedUserId' for authorization independence, linking to a Firebase user's UID for self-service access. Admins have full access; linked users can read their own record.

params.name: customerId
params.description: Unique identifier for a specific customer.

definition.description: Records monthly invoices generated for customers. Includes denormalized 'customerLinkedUserId' for authorization independence, allowing linked customer users to read their own invoices directly. Admins have full access; linked users can read their own invoices.

params.name: invoiceId
params.description: Unique identifier for a specific invoice.

firestore.reasoning:
The "NetInvoice Offline" application requires a robust Firestore structure to manage customer data, service packages, and invoices, while supporting both administrative staff access and potential future customer self-service. Given the 'offline-first' nature with IndexedDB and the need for 'realtime' online synchronization, the design prioritizes scalability, security, and ease of debugging. 

1.  **Authorization Independence via Denormalization (CRITICAL):**
    *   To eliminate the need for costly and complex `get()` calls in Firestore Security Rules and ensure atomic operations, authorization context is denormalized. 
    *   For the `Customer` entity, a `linkedUserId` field (nullable string) will be added. This field will store the Firebase `uid` of the user account associated with that customer. This enables direct `request.auth.uid == resource.data.linkedUserId` checks in rules for self-service access.
    *   For the `Invoice` entity, in addition to `customerId`, a `customerLinkedUserId` field (nullable string) will be denormalized from the `Customer`'s `linkedUserId`. This allows security rules on invoices to directly verify `request.auth.uid == resource.data.customerLinkedUserId` without needing to fetch the parent `Customer` document, ensuring Authorization Independence.

2.  **Structural Segregation & QAPs (Query As You Pay):**
    *   All core entities (`ServicePackage`, `Customer`, `Invoice`) will reside in dedicated top-level collections: `/servicePackages`, `/customers`, and `/invoices`. This ensures a homogeneous security posture within each collection, simplifying security rules and enabling efficient queries.
    *   **`/servicePackages/{servicePackageId}`:** All documents here will share read access for authenticated users (both staff and customers to view package details) and write access restricted to administrators. This allows for simple QAP-compliant `list` operations.
    *   **`/customers/{customerId}`:** Administrators can `list` all customer records. For self-service, customers can efficiently query their own record using `customers.where('linkedUserId', '==', request.auth.uid)`. The `linkedUserId` field is essential for this QAP-compliant query and rule enforcement.
    *   **`/invoices/{invoiceId}`:** Administrators can `list` all invoice records. For self-service, customers can efficiently query their own invoices using `invoices.where('customerLinkedUserId', '==', request.auth.uid)`. The `customerLinkedUserId` field facilitates this QAP-compliant query and rule enforcement without cross-document reads.

3.  **Database Access Control (DBAC):**
    *   Administrative roles will be managed using a dedicated collection, e.g., `/roles_admin/{adminUid}`. The existence of a document at this path for `request.auth.uid` will grant administrative privileges, following the "Existence over Content" principle for clear and simple role-based access control. This collection is a meta-entity for authorization and not included in the primary entity paths below.

This structure ensures that the application's real-time and online database components are secure, performant, and maintainable, fully supporting the required features while adhering to best practices for Firestore security and scalability.
